functional_pass_evidence: Selectors found active list title, four product rows, search input, and estimated cost text.
No console error was observed in the triggering runtime pass.
design_anchors: Shopping-list UI should be dense enough for repeated grocery planning.
Primary list, search, and cost comparison should read as one workflow.
Visual hierarchy should make item state, next action, and total cost scannable.
Surfaces should look product-specific, not default scaffold output.

visible_findings.evidence: Rows consume most of the viewport while showing only name and 1 szt.
visible_findings.repair_direction: Use compact list density with quantity controls and status affordances.

visible_findings.evidence: Search and explore controls sit in a separate block below the active list.
visible_findings.repair_direction: Integrate product discovery with the active shopping workflow.

visible_findings.evidence: Headers, list items, quick preview, and open-list action compete without a clear scan path.
visible_findings.repair_direction: Establish primary, secondary, and action hierarchy around the list.

visible_findings.evidence: Large beige slabs and dashed links read as default scaffolding rather than a polished shopping app.
visible_findings.repair_direction: Use intentional surfaces, spacing, and affordances tied to the app domain.

visible_findings.evidence: The cost slab overlaps the list card visually and the page lacks cohesive workflow composition.
visible_findings.repair_direction: Recompose the screen so list, search, and cost comparison form a coherent product experience.
